mnemonics: HI-FIVES for Database Evolution: Hierarchical, IMS, Flat-file, Inverted-list, V.S. Codasyl, Entity-Relationship, SQL
keyPoints: Started with flat-file systems leading to data redundancy and inconsistency.
Hierarchical (IMS) and Network (CODASYL) models provided early structure but were complex.
The relational model, proposed by E.F. Codd, revolutionized DBMS with simplicity and a strong theoretical foundation, leading to the development of SQL.
commonMistakes: Thinking the relational model was the first database model (hierarchical and network came before).
Believing SQL is a database model (it is a language used primarily with the relational model).
